title: Praise
text: ‘The first great masterpiece of the globally warmed generation. Here is an American classic which, at a stroke, makes McCarthy a contender for the Nobel Prize for Literature.’ Andrew O’Hagan ‘ The Road is about tenderness despite everything. McCarthy has unmade the world, but against all the odds, this novel holds out a thin strand of hope: for the power of language and storytelling even at the end of all things’ Financial Times ‘McCarthy conjures from this pitiless flight the miracle of unswerving humanity. Gripping beyond belief.’ Chris Cleave, Sunday Telegraph ‘So good that it will devour you. It is incandescent.’ Daily Telegraph ‘Trenchant and terrifying, written with stripped-down urgency and fuelled by the force of a universal nightmare . . . This is art that both frightens and inspires’ New York Times ‘You will read on, absolutely convinced, thrilled, mesmerized. All the modern novel can do is done here.’ Alan Warner, Guardian ‘A masterpiece that will soon be considered a classic.’ Herald ‘We are watching a late flowering of a great American novelist. For all its grim imaginings, The Road ’s divine language carries its two entwined souls above the darkness. McCarthy continues to carry the fire’ Independent on Sunday ‘A spectacular novel . . . Images continue to recur long after it has been put aside’ Scotland on Sunday ‘A gripping, heart-rending human story, which explores the depths of despair and savagery beside the heights of love, tenderness and self-sacrifice. McCarthy has delivered a master stroke: this tale is beautiful, hypnotic, and terrifyingly real’ New Statesman ‘No novelist working today is better suited to chronicle the end of the world than Cormac McCarthy . . . What really elevates the novel from its slough of despond is the sheer, terrible beauty of the writing’ Sunday Times ‘McCarthy shows that he is one of the greatest American writers alive’ Times Literary Supplement
